mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows and forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months despite the unemployment spike, invalidating the earnings compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting margin expansion and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized despite rising industrial production, breaking the margin expansion assumption.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 0.5% combined with stable employment growth creates an ultra-loose financial environment that forces portfolio rebalancing into equities and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline by more than 2% over the subsequent 5 trading days in 70% of triggered instances, contradicting the liquidity-driven rebalancing mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate profit forecasts and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in more than half of the quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, or the predictive IC remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during a late-cycle expansion phase signals peak labor market tightness that precedes wage-driven margin compression and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when unemployment falls below 5.5% in late-cycle conditions, disproving the wage-push margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates a sudden supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate output and triggers immediate negative equity market reactions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in over 65% of months following a 0.5% industrial production decline, invalidating the supply contraction shock hypothesis.
